content: Should we plan to replace our own cstr with https://doc.rust-lang.org/nightly/core/ffi/struct.CStr.html (which is new-ish)

Yeah, after we get to upgrade the compiler, we can give it a go (CString too).

However, if we want to still implement Display, it may be a problem -- I think it was what triggered the chat I had with @bjorn3 as well as @Gary Guo in one of the informal meetings back in summer about the possibility of maybe relaxing the trait coherence rules (and creating "coherence domains"), but that wouldn't help for the standard library (unless we carve some small exceptions that we know will never conflict I guess).
After discussing it on the rust zulip someone shared an example where coherence domains would cause specialization to break.
For CStr we could write an extension trait that adds a .display() method returning a type implementing Display. Libstd uses such a .display() method for Path too.
